working: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 remains the lift champion: +66.5% vs baseline (17.0 to 28.3 daily sessions) with 53 direct sessions in the 3 days after the 5/19 send.
Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3) drove +65.2% lift (21.0 to 34.7 daily sessions) and 49 direct sessions in 3 days, proving the reminder-send tactic works.
Open rates stay far above the 25-35% health benchmark: the two most recent sends (7/2) opened at 48.6% (Alumni) and 50.1% (Staff-Client, 8,894 sends); program average is 52.0% across 100 campaigns.

Referring Partners eNews - June 2026 (6/29) has the best click-to-open rate of the window at 4.51% and now shows +10.5% lift, up from the -25.3% reading in the 7/6 run as its post-send window matured.
Alumni eNews - May 2026 holds the best click rate of the window at 1.79% (CTOR 3.77%), a content template worth studying.
not_working: UTM leakage is total: all 10 tracked sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d runs 21-53; GA4 credited Email only 2 sessions in 90 days (0.1%) because newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so Direct (960 sessions, 49.0%) absorbs the email traffic.

The 7/2 sends (Alumni July + Staff-Client June) sit at -25.3% lift (17.0 baseline to 12.7 daily sessions), likely dragged down by the July 4th holiday weekend; only 21 direct sessions followed in 3 days.
Click rates are soft: 8 of 10 sends fall below 1.2%, with the 7/2 pair at 0.78% and 0.76%, well under the ~2% program average.
GSC 7-day window (7/1-7/8) shows only 3 clicks on 1,868 impressions with impression-weighted position at 23.45, down from 17.56 and 3,173 impressions in the 7/6 run.
Organic Social is stuck at 2.4% of sessions (47 of 1,961), and Referring Partners eNews opens at 25.6%, half the program average.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email to all newsletter links; every one of the 10 tracked sends shows 0 attributed email sessions, so this remains the single highest-leverage attribution fix.
Avoid holiday-adjacent sends: both 7/2 sends read -25.3% lift, while the two +65% lifts came on Tue 5/19 and Wed 6/3; anchor future sends to mid-week, early-to-mid month, away from holidays.
Repromote the Staff-Client format and the Alumni reminder tactic; they produced the only +65% lifts and the largest 3-day direct spikes (53 and 49 sessions).

Investigate the GSC slide (position 17.56 to 23.45, impressions 3,173 to 1,868) before the next run; confirm whether it is a data-lag artifact of the short 7/1-7/8 window or a real ranking loss on tracked keywords.
Rework the Referring Partners subject-line approach: its 25.6% open rate lags the 52% program average even though its 4.51% CTOR shows the content resonates once opened.
summary: Traffic is flat vs the 7/6 run (1,961 vs 1,959 sessions over 90 days) with Direct at 49.0% and Organic Search at 39.8%. Email attribution remains fully broken (0 UTM-tagged sessions across all 10 sends), the 7/2 holiday-week sends read -25.3% lift, and the GSC 7-day position slipped to 23.5 from 17.6.
